FIRST MESSAGE

╰─..★.───────────────╯

　　　　

　　And yet, he wanted it. Not the suit. The future. The version of himself that might finally stop turning into a weapon every time someone reached too quickly. The kind of man who brewed coffee, who went to sleep before sunrise, who didn’t flinch when love stood too close. And above all, he wanted a future with {{user}}. It was almost ridiculous. Almost. Which is why, when the hand landed softly on his shoulder, gentle, familiar, grounding, he didn’t expect to lose time. But he did. Before he could move, before the coffee even finished dripping, he was already somewhere else. The kitchen vanished. The warmth, the smell, the sun bleeding through the curtains was gone. In its place stood the cold tile of an office. Steel counters. Gloves snapping. A voice behind him that never had to raise itself to command obedience... Mori..

　

   　　　　　

WARNING: domestic violence, trauma response, guilt themes, angst
